segments.text: Job replied,
I've heard all this before. You are comforters who just cause trouble! [^87]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will your windy speeches never cease?[^88] What's bothering you that you have to answer me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I could talk like you do if you were in my place, stringing words together to criticize you, ridiculing you with a toss of my head.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I could build you up by my mouth speaking words; the movement of my lips would ease your pain. [^89]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For me, if I speak it doesn't ease my pain, and if I don't speak the pain is still there.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God, [^90] you have worn me out. You have destroyed my whole family.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You have made me shrivel up, which is a witness against me; my thin body testifies against me. [^91]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has torn me apart in his anger; in his hostility he has gnashed his teeth at me; my enemy pierces me with his fierce look.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: People stare at me with their mouths wide open, they slap me on my cheeks to mock me, they crowd around me to attack me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God has handed me over to evil people; he has thrown me into their hands.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I was living in peace and he smashed me to pieces. He grabbed me by the neck and dashed me to pieces. He set me up as his target.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: His archers surround me. His arrows pierce my kidneys without mercy. He pours out my gall on the ground.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Like a wall he breaks me down, breach upon breach, rushing at me like a warrior.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have sown sackcloth[^92] to cover my skin; my strength lies broken in the dust. [^93]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My face is red from crying and dark shadows surround my eyes,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: although I have done nothing wrong[^94] and my prayer is pure.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Earth, do not cover up my blood. May my cry find no place to hide.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look, right now my witness is in heaven; the one who speaks for me is on high.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My friends scorn me, but my eyes pour out tears to God.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I want my witness to speak for me with God as someone does for their friend.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For in a few short years I shall go down that road from which I shall not return.
segments.speaker: Job
